videos.title: 10 Things you should NOT do in Jordan - Travel Guide
videos.description:
Jordan is a Must-See! The beautiful desert, the ancient city Petra, & the bustling capital Amman will leave you stunned and craving for more... and there is so much more! 
BUT be careful when you travel to Jordan, because there is a few things that you should not do!

videos.transcript: [Music] don't think the country is cheap now yeah you can get good food that's cheap if you just want to go for falafel and hummus but it can get quite expensive especially the accommodation now for a hostel you pay around 15 to 20 per night and if you want to have a hotel room it very easily goes to 50 60 70 100 per night so don't make the mistake of just thinking it's a very cheap country bear that in mind and just look for good deals online before you go there don't forget to book the tours before you go to the buddy rum desert now if you want to do a tour through the vadiram you know you're sleeping in one of those camps you want to do a tour book one of those tours in the experiences beforehand because if you arrive at the camp and you know you want to haggle it won't work do the before see where they have the best prices maybe you know get together with some people do together that will pull down the price don't just shake hands with the women there wait if they offer you the hand it's a muslim country so a lot of the women there don't shake hands with men so you know be respectful and if the woman offers you to shake the hand shake the hand otherwise don't just you know point your your hand to the woman because you know maybe they don't want to do that don't forget to haggle now it's an arab country where a lot of people haggle it's very normal especially when you go to the markets so don't forget to do that because the price that you hear in the beginning is not the price that in the end you have to pay or that people pay it's normal you know to haggle and and talk about the price now one tip i can give you is to speak to locals and ask them maybe your guide or the hotel you know like i want to buy the scarf at the market how much should i pay what's normal because then you have an idea of you know going to the market of how much it should cost and you know how much you can haggle don't try diving in the dead sea now it's very salty and diving is not possible because of the buoyancy it's 34 salt which just will make your eyes burn when you try to dive into it how do i know i tried it not a good idea so don't do that another thing is to be careful you know when you shave before because um all these little wounds you know when you go in the dead sea the salt will come in and it will burn like crazy don't shave before don't try to dive otherwise just enjoy floating in the dead sea don't think jordan is just desert and hot now it is desert and it can get hot but also it can get quite cold especially in the desert at night so bring something warm and there's other places there's even areas where it snows so just keep that in mind there's a lot of variety you know the red sea with beautiful beaches the desert of course some mountains lush and nice canyons so much to see a lot of variety not just desert and super hot don't forget to buy the jordan pass before you visit the country now now this pass covers the visa fee the entrance to petra and the entrance to a lot of other sites a lot of people think you can get the jordan pass at the airport but that's wrong you have to get it before so don't make that mistake don't fall for the scams especially at petra there's a lot of tourists coming and when there's a lot of tourists you know people sometimes try to scan them let's just say it how it is unfortunately and one of those scams is the horseback rides now it will say in your ticket that the horseback rides are included and they technically are but the people will expect a hefty tip so i recommend you not to do it plus it's just a short walk anyways don't do it because you will start arguing with the people there if you say it's free and it's just a very unpleasant experience furthermore you know there's a lot of things you can buy in in petra and sometimes they will put a wristband on your arm and say it's free but then you know they want you to give them money just try to be aware and especially in the touristy places such as petra avoid the scams and don't fall for them if you're going to jordan don't feel uncomfortable if the guy grabs your hand to just show you around now this is especially for guys men there you know they hold hands while they walk around especially when they get along well now it's nothing to do with being gay or whatsoever it's just the way it's done there may be a bit uncomfortable for us but you know if you really want to take a deep dive into the culture just go along with it happened to me several times the guy just took the hand it feels a bit uncomfortable but it's just the way it's done there now last but not least don't forget to say shukran this is maybe one of the most important things at least that's what i think shukran means thank you and it's always nice you know people always appreciate when you just learn a little bit of their language and i mean to say thank you that's the least you can do shukran thank you very easy to remember people will be happy if you know a bit and if you keep those things in mind i guarantee you you will have a fantastic fantastic time in jordan it's a beautiful country so just don't do these 10 things and you will have a fine and fantastic time

safetyNotes: BUT be careful when you travel to Jordan, because there is a few things that you should not do

videos.title: 15 BEST Things to do in Jordan in 2026 (Amman, Dead Sea, Wadi Rum, & More) 🇯🇴

There are so many places around Jordan with such a diverse set of landscapes from the Dead Sea to Wadi Rum and the unbelievable Petra, you will get lost in a land that is so different from other places in the world. videos.transcript: [Music] to say it simply Jordan is one of the most exotic countries I have ever visited the country is filled with so much history culture and natural beauty that feels truly like another planet Jordan sits in the heart of the Middle East with a population of 10 million people inside Jordan you can find places like the ancient Petra Martian likee Landscapes known as W rum and of course the Dead Sea but what truly set set Jordan apart is the warmth and Hospitality of its people mixed with the rich Traditions that makes every visit one you'll never forget in today's video we will be sharing with you the 15 best things you can do in Jordan and they are not in any specific order from best to worst so make sure you stick around until the end so you don't miss out on any of these amazing things to do in Jordan let's Jump Right In number one on the list is to take a trip to the Dead Sea you are now in the lowest point on the earth oh lowest point on the this is one of the most exotic places you may have ever experienced do not jump or Dive Into the Dead Sea enter until you can squat then lie back gently do not immerse your head ooh oh it's squishy wo bro feel the texture on that oh my God all right so we're just going to swimming in a heavily concentrated salt sea is so different than a normal swimming pool and you will find that out quickly with how effortlessly easy it is to float without moving a muscle in your body and this is due to an extreme concentration of salt I'm not moving a single muscle in my body and normally you would just sink and hit the ground but I'm just floating I'm almost on top of the water but aside from that the Dead Sea has therapeutic properties that people from all over the world come to experience covering yourself in mud and then floating is actually a wellness ritual that is like a natural spa and it was for sure one of my favorite things I did while in Jordan basically out here in the um area by the Dead Sea it's just kind of resort it's not a city like a man so you can't just call Uber you can't easily get a taxi now we know next time if you're coming out here I'd honestly recommend to rent a car number two on the list is to make a stop over at Point de view it almost looks like snow when you're first walking up to it but I'll tell you guys that it's definitely not snow look at that these are just chunks chunks of salt it's a full block of salt mhm that's certainly salt guys wo look at this what in the heck there are a lot of flies here if you just watch closely that's the only obnoxious part right now basically when your feet get wet with a salt and then along with this dirt to it it mixes in and it makes it a bit muddy and when you're trying to climb up a steep incline basically my foot just slides right out Harry's got the right idea he's got the shoes on Smart lad this Viewpoint is along the coastline of the Dead Sea and the drive there is an experience in itself the later hours of the afternoon it's crazy cuz it's only like maybe around 300 p.m. but it feels like it's like 5 just with uh how early it gets dark at this time of the year Point day view is a great place to visit in the afternoon towards sunset time as the Landscapes look the most beautiful at that time with the Golden Glow number three on the list is to see the King Abdullah mosque situated in a man and then on the other the road we have a pretty large church or Monastery that's cool that they have both of them right next to each other so you want to visit the mosque yeah yes please but you need to wear something because you two you wear shorts you can go like this what do I have to wear it's called nice to cover okay actually I want to wear one too okay okay perfect the King Abdullah mask is an iconic place of worship and a landmark for the city which speaks to Jordan's cultural and religious Heritage such mindblowing architecture every single part of this entire place has so much detail this huge mosque displays Jordanian tradition Edition mixed in with modern architecture and The prominent Blue Dome captures your attention wherever you are in the city you can see the uh shape of it just right above the bridge there and you have the iconic blue architecture that just pops out number four on the list is to visit the Aman cadel you can use the uh Jordan pass and that actually gets you your entrance covered when you walk in so otherwise you pay I think about three Jordanian dinar and if you're a Jordanian resident you pay about 25 cents so it's not too bad The Citadel sits up on a hill overlooking much of a man making it the place you want to spend your evening during sunset the archaeological site offers a historical Journey back in time and is home to ancient ruins which tells the story of centuries gone by the panoramic views of am man from up here are a splendid site and it's the best time to be up there during prayer call you'll be able to see the birds flying by the beautiful sunset and a very peaceful evening in am man oh that was an amazing quick little 30 45 minutes up here at the Citadel guys at Sunset really an amazing time to come up here because the prayer calls the peacefulness of seeing the city start to change from the busyness of the day to the calm and peacefulness of the night as the lights slowly come on one by one and you can just start to see the Silhouettes of this place up here at the top of the mountain so but now we're cruising towards uh rainbow Street if you guys saw in the last video we went there we kind of like walked through there during the day basically flowers and light fixtures running across leading to a shisha bar on this side a modern Cafe on the other side so those was the flowers we just walked past over here we have baskets hanging down we'll take you guys here at night cuz apparently that's when it's most alive you've got a bunch of like food stands down there there's those beautiful lights that I Mars looking landscapes YouTube brother yeah oh yeah brother and you can also find many things like souvenirs coffee tea and a bunch of other things inside of their Shop number 10 on the list is to the best way to experience it is by taking a Jeep tour through the desert all right here we go our man's is really going into second gear now we're getting some solid speed full sand Arrow baby so years and now it just looks like this otherworldly type thing cruising through the desert at high speeds in this otherworldly like atmosphere was one of the biggest highlights of my trip to Jordan This is insane we're cruising in through Mars number 11 on the list is to Camp inside of w rum one of the best camping experiences I have ever had and these are the types of Domes that we're going to Wind Fire now if you're coming here in High season around the December uh January time frame then you'll pay a little bit more you'll pay about $600 for uh two people to stay in one of these places number 12 on the list is to check out the cave bar all right this is the cave bar then yeah o warmed up a little bit in here cave bar is one of the places you can get yourself a nice big beer if you're into that but it is expensive ironic that the only place that SES beer is inside a Historical Museum yeah exactly it's good with his taste buds problem is they're 10 bucks a beer so I'm going to end up drinking two or three of those it will be an expensive night cave bar is tucked within the ancient city of Petra and offers a unique venue for relaxation so we just changed over to this cave guys we got a good spot here we got the fires going as you can imagine by the name cave bar is carved into the Rocks which makes the atmosphere so unique compared to most places you would imagine having an ice cold brew and once again if you're not into that there is also food as well as shisha or arele options paying about $100 for us to have three pints and a solid meal along with a shisha you know being inside a Petra that's kind of what you expect $50 per person to be able to down it is what it is so I can see why our guy said cave bar is expensive it's going to be inside Petra number 13 on the list is to visit akaba akaba sits down in the southern part of Jordan and is located right on the Red Sea you got boats all in the distance and you have the hayy mountain the place that we're at uh we paid $400 for two nights split freeways so 200 bucks a night it's like 70 bucks per person per night but you know Resort all the amenities this city is different from anywhere else we have showed you so far as it is more of a resort town you can find yourself at a nice resort to stay out like we did and enjoy drinks Sunset and amazing pools you can also go and cruise around the city and see all of the amazing things akaba has to offer seems like you can get a lot of stuff in this market everything from like clothes and souvenirs to you got all types of food look at this look at this there's always sunshine in my cart how much for this one 10 if the spelling was correct I'd do 10 JD but the spelling was off so it's a hard one over here we got Joseph's liquor store so the you can buy booze down here pretty much anywhere you got beer options you got wine options you got alcohol the nice part is you can get pretty much anything you need in a very small area like in the last I don't know 2 hours or so we went from eating food to walking around a uh area here where all the locals are hanging out to doing some market Hunts buying some cloth the best way to describe this place is more of a Beach Town type of feel number 14 on the list is to go snorkeling in the Red Sea the Red Sea is renowned for its vibrant marine life and Alive coral reefs you you can rent a boat for a decent price we' got the captain spot back here we've got the glass bottom so we might be able to see a couple fish couple pieces of coral down there you can also rent like Yachts if you want to spend a little bit more money we've got a small group so we're doing this for about 100 Jordanian dinar which is like $140 and over here you can see we've got some fairies we've got some tugboats a lot a lot going on in this Harbor we're not actually sure who she is but uh she's on the boat with us now and as you can see on this side we have a whole another country so there's a lot you can uh explore we're pretty close look wow you can almost touch the coral how is that possible various sets of Shades you got dark blue and as it gets shallower light blue then you can start to see some of the coral here and I will tell you locals told us after we rented a boat that we paid far too much from ours so make sure you learn from our mistakes so this last part of the boat ride got a little bit dicey because we were trying to figure out the price with what we found out later was the boat owner so we agreed on the price with him which was the initial amount 100 Jordanian dinar or $140 but the random lady that was on the boat she was like demanding extra money from us like an extra 20 JD it was just like a random person that showed up on the boat that we were never told about girl that was on the boat was trying to tell us we needed to pay 20 more for some reason I literally have no idea why we would pay 20 more because her boss whoever the guy was we were on the phone with who apparently is the boat manager AG on the price with us for 130 so they're trying to get us to pay 50 but the good news is is there are many different boat options and companies that can offer you a boat trip and or snorkeling and you can even go scuba diving if you're interested in that and last but not least number 15 on the list is to try out Jordanian Cuisine some of the tastiest food I have had in the Middle East has been in Jordan as there are such a Rich Blend of flavors in all of the different foods that I tried the dish known as fakar went to go ahead and break into the traditional Jordan Anan food right here yeah often prepared with slow cooked lamb and aromatic slices is a culinary delight for those seeking authentic tastes and want the traditional experience of true Jordanian food this is just one example but there are so many options and we tried out a ton of them when we were in aan and I loved the food in Jordan and so that is where we are going to go ahead and end this video today guys I will include links to some of the things we did in the description below as well as some other things that you can find to do in Jordan that we didn't mention the video so thank you guys so much for watching as always and we will see you in the next video

videos.title: 12 BEST Things to Do in Amman, Jordan | Travel Guide
videos.description:
How to find cheap flights and travel more: https://travelforalmostfree.com/ref=beforeyougo2 // Here are the best things to do in Amman, Jordan.

-------------------
Recommended Tours in Amman:

3-Day Private Tour from Amman: Petra, Wadi Rum, Dana, Aqaba, and Dead Sea: https://www.viator.com/tours/Amman/Three-Days-Tour-Petra-Wadi-Rum-Gulf-of-Aqaba-and-Dead-Sea/d5503-18249P10?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-ammam-desc

Multi day tour in Jordan - 08 Days: https://www.viator.com/tours/Amman/Jordan-Tour-in-08-Days/d5503-124334P1?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-ammam-desc

Private Tour of Ajlun Castle and Jerash: https://www.viator.com/tours/Amman/Private-Tour-of-Ajlun-and-Jerash/d5503-50208P7?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-ammam-desc

Private Street Food and Walking Tour Amman Downtown: https://www.viator.com/tours/Amman/Private-Street-Food-and-Walking-Tour-Amman-Downtown/d5503-107508P3?pid=P00104625&mcid=42383&medium=link&medium_version=selector&campaign=yt-ammam-desc
-------------------

Welcome to this exciting travel guide where we’ll be exploring the beautiful city of Amman, Jordan. Amman is the capital of Jordan and is a perfect blend of old and new, where modernity meets tradition. It is a vibrant city with a rich history, ancient ruins, delicious food, and friendly people. In this video, we’ll be taking you through some of the best things to do in Amman.

Our first stop is the ancient Citadel, which sits atop a hill and offers stunning panoramic views of the city. The Citadel is home to some of the oldest ruins in Amman, including the Temple of Hercules and the Umayyad Palace. It's a great place to explore the history of the city and take some amazing photos.

Next up, we have the Roman Amphitheatre, which is a must-visit for history buffs. This impressive structure can seat up to 6,000 people and is one of the most well-preserved Roman amphitheatres in the world. Take a walk around and soak up the atmosphere or climb up to the top for a bird's eye view of the surrounding area.

For those who love to shop, the Downtown Souk is a great place to visit. It's a bustling market full of vendors selling everything from spices to handicrafts. You can find some great souvenirs to take home, and the prices are very reasonable. Make sure to haggle for the best deals!

If you're looking for some relaxation, a visit to the Turkish Bathhouses is highly recommended. These traditional bathhouses offer a unique experience where you can soak in hot water and enjoy a massage. It's a great way to unwind after a day of sightseeing.

Another great place to visit is Rainbow Street, which is known for its trendy cafes and restaurants. It's a great place to grab a bite to eat and people-watch. The street is lined with colorful buildings and has a lively atmosphere, especially in the evenings.

Finally, we have the Jordan Museum, which is a must-visit for anyone interested in the history of Jordan. The museum houses artifacts and exhibits from the Paleolithic period up to modern times. It's a great way to learn about the country's rich history and culture.

That's it for our guide to Amman! We hope you enjoyed our recommendations and found them useful for your next trip. Amman is a beautiful city with so much to offer, and we're sure you'll fall in love with it just like we did. Thanks for watching, and don't forget to subscribe to our channel for more travel guides!

Keep watching to see the top things to do in the Amman, top attractions in Amman, places to visit in the Amman and Middle East travel guides.

videos.transcript: welcome to Amman the captivating capital city of Jordan where ancient history effortlessly blends with Modern urban Vibes here are the top things to do in Amman Jordan number 12. Roman Theater One of A month's must-see attractions is the Roman Theater a second century Marvel showcasing the Grandeur and sophistication of ancient Roman Amphitheater architecture constructed between 138 and 161 CE under Emperor Antonius Pius this historic Monument offers not only a glimpse into Jordan's past but also serves as an important cultural Hub in modern times visiting the Roman Theater allows travelers to immerse themselves in history while experiencing artistic events reflecting Jordan's vibrant culture many cultural and historical attractions in Amman take place at this Amphitheater ranging from musical performances to concerts and events number 11. Amman Citadel nestled atop a hill in downtown Oman you'll find the breathtaking Aman City an attraction teaming with historical and Architectural wonders this iconic site has witnessed numerous civilizations throughout history evident through the captivating blend of Roman Byzantine and umayad remains that Adorn its landscape from The Towering Columns of the Temple of Hercules to the well-preserved umayyad Palace there is much to see and explore this ancient site the panoramic views of the city from The Citadel are breathtaking and not to be missed don't miss out on a visit to the Jordan archaeological Museum within the Citadel grounds here you'll uncover ancient Treasures such as Pottery artifacts and coins dating back thousands of years perfect for travelers eager about delving into Jordan's Rich cultural past number 10. Jordan Museum the Jordan Museum located in the heart of Oman stands tall as a cultural and historical attraction that offers Travelers an authentic look at the nation's Rich past as you explore the various galleries within this top-notch institution you'll be taken on a chronological Journey from prehistoric times to Islamic history and modern day Jordan one standout is the archaeological and historical Gallery the Museum's largest display housing fascinating Scale Models alongside significant relics found throughout the region to gain insights into traditional Jordanian customs and folklore make sure to visit downtown amman's folklore Museum as well number nine darit Alpha noon if you're an art lover or a history buff make sure to add Dara elfu noon to your itinerary when visiting Amman established in 1988 the cultural Haven promotes Contemporary Art and supports artists from Jordan and the Arab world dart elfanoon is well known for its outstanding Museum practices that have earned recognition from the Khalid shomon foundation in Jordan you'll find yourself surrounded by excellent architecture and artistic expression as you explore the galleries showcasing sculptures paintings and other forms of Contemporary Art curated by some of the most talented artists in the region number 8. all belade area a trip to Oman would not be complete without visiting the historic all-balad area this bustling Marketplace is located in the Heart of the City and offers a true Taste of local culture aside from shopping all palad also boasts some stunning examples of historical architecture like umayad Palace and the Grand husseini Mosque that are worth exploring further take a stroll along its narrow streets lined with cafes where you can sit back for a while to take in the sights and sounds around you and don't forget to try out some traditional Jordanian street food like Falafel or amanaish number 7. caser al-qarana one striking cultural and historical attraction in a mod that you should visit is kaser alcarana nestled in the Eastern desert of Jordan This Palace has a harsh and Barren moonscape appearance that may seem inhospitable for human habitation however it's one of the well-preserved desert castles that can be visited along with kasar al-azrock and kucer amra they're isolated from Human settlement located just an hour away from the city today you can explore its intricately decorated rooms that showcase Islamic Art and motifs carved into stucco plaster walls you can also admire its Central Courtyard that features esoteric symmetrical patterns made from colored stones set into white marble slabs number six King Abdullah mosque located in the heart of Oman King Abdullah mosque is a stunning historical and religious attraction that should be on every traveler's itinerary this mosque was built as a tribute to the late King Hussein's grandfather and it features magnificent Islamic architecture with intricate carvings and blue Mosaic domes the mosque is not only an important pilgrimage site but also a popular tourist destination allowing non-muslim visitors to Marvel at its beauty and learn about Islamic traditions and Customs while appreciating Jordan's Rich history number five rainbow Street rainbow street is a vibrant and bustling Street in the heart of Oman known for its colorful buildings Lively atmosphere and eclectic mix of shops cafes and restaurants the streets stretches from the first circle to the Mango Street and is lined with Boutique shops art galleries bookstores and souvenir shops selling traditional Jordanian handicrafts the street comes alive at night with its many restaurants and cafes offering a variety of Cuisines from traditional Middle Eastern dishes to International Cuisine and live music performances rainbow street is also a popular spot for young locals and tourists alike with its vibrant nightlife and Social Scene number four Wadi mujib one of the most popular outdoor activities in Amman is exploring Wadi mujif a natural reserve with awe-inspiring hiking trails and canyoning experiences the reserve features massive Gorges that are created by free-flowing streams making it a Haven for adventure Seekers who love to explore nature at its best one must-do activity when visiting wadimut is taking on the canyon hike which takes between two to four hours to complete but brings fantastic memories and unique experiences such as rock climbing swimming and Hiking through rushing rivers in a stunning Slot Canyon for experienced climbers and seasoned hikers looking for more challenges they may want to try out Ibex Trail at Wadi mujip another popular outdoor activity featuring climbing up the bed of the mujib River from its mouth number three gerish if you're a history buff you don't want to miss visiting jarish on your trip to Jordan located just under an hour's drive from Amman cherish is widely regarded as one of the best preserved Roman provincial cities in the world as you wander this ancient city you might imagine what life was like during the time of Rome's Heyday some iconic landmarks in jairish to check out include the temple Artemis Hadrian's Arch and the oval Plaza the grand Hippodrome is perhaps the most impressive structure which could seat up to 15 000 Spectators and was used for chariot races and other events you can also explore the city's ancient theaters the Byzantine era church of Saint Theodore and the nymphium an ornate Fountain dedicated to the nymphs jarish even has a museum that showcases the city's history and artifacts so if you want to experience a unique glimpse into the ancient world be sure to add cherish to your itinerary number two Dead Sea visit one of the most popular day trips from Amman is a visit to the Dead Sea the high salt content of the lake creates a unique ecosystem and provides health benefits for visitors famous for its healing properties visitors can cover themselves in mud rich with minerals known to rejuvenate skin cells and improve one's overall appearance along the Jordan Valley Highway that leads to the Dead Sea you'll find several viewpoints where you can Marvel its stunning views of this natural wonder number one Cave of the seven sleepers located east of Amman the Cave of the seven sleepers is a historical and religious site that should definitely be on your list when visiting Jordan's capital according to the quranic story Surah al-qa this cave was where seven young men sought Refuge during a time of persecution for their beliefs visiting the cave today you'll find it holds seven small Graves and an eerie collection of human bones reminders of its intriguing history the area around the cave can be rough terrain wise so make sure you wear comfortable walking shoes when venturing out to explore if Islamic sites are your thing consider making a half day trip from Amman to visit this historical site along with other significant locations such as Alka mosque click here for more things to do in Jordan foreign [Music]

videos.transcript: [Music] so you're going to jordan so one of the things you should do beforehand is getting the jordan pass now this jordan pass costs 100 or more depending on you know what kind of package you get and it pays for the fee for the entrance for fee for entrance of petra you know the archaeological site which you probably want to see while you're there and a lot of other sites so price value is perfect it's really really good to get this beforehand and do this before because you can't get it at the airport and you buy it online you get a qr code and then they scan it when you enter the country and again you don't have to pay the visa fee you don't have to pay the entrance for petra and depending on how many days you stay in petra you know just one two or three it costs a little bit more but it starts at 100 great price value so get the jordan pass the best time to visit jordan is between march and may and from october till december because in summer it can get very very hot so it can get quite unbearable now bear in mind i was there in december and it was already getting quite cold so definitely bring a jacket because you don't want to freeze it's not just desert and even in the desert in the night it gets cold now when it comes to timing you might travel during ramadan which is the holy month where everybody fasts and it's still possible to travel jordan it's no problem just be respectful during this time muslims don't eat and drink until the sun goes down so don't just eat and drink in public or in front of people hotel restaurants will still be open so you still will be able to get served some food there's a lot of tourists so you know not to worry about you're not going to be the only one but just keep in mind that maybe some attractions close a bit earlier because people want to get home to to eat and break their fast now when it comes to eating and drinking jordan is one of the best places to be mansoff is like their national most traditional dish and consists of lamb and rice and it's commonly found on pretty much every menu and you can also find hummus on falafel everywhere which is cheap and it's just so good now one of the restaurants i recommend you to go innaman is called hashim very famous the king was even there very basic food and you know you just sit on these little tables where they bring you falafel and hummus very cheap fantastic food you gotta go there everybody in jordan knows it it's it's not a secret tip anymore but it's very good really really recommended now you will definitely enjoy the drinks there and i don't mean the alcoholic ones because it's a muslim country but i mean coffee and tea now the jordanian tea is typically an infusion of black tea with added herbs and it can be sage mint or others and it's really a very very nice taste now jordanians drink their tea and coffee very sweet so if you don't want you know a lot of sugar added just mention that beforehand now jordan is a relatively compact country you can travel kind of everywhere in like four to five hours so it's very easy to see a lot of jordan in a short amount of time for independent travelers and who are a bit more adventurous you know there's the option to rent a car and drive around yourself of course it's different you got to be careful especially in amman but it's still possible it's not as crazy as you would think of now the other option is to rent a driver or a taxi or use the public transport now just bear in mind the public transport is different than maybe in the western world for example when you take a bus the bus won't leave until the bus is full so there is no set time you could be waiting for you know 15 minutes if you're lucky or for an hour and a half like i did when i was there another great option for travelers are the jet buses now these are buses designated for tourists they leave on a specific time so you know that it's gonna leave and you know when it's going to arrive at your destination and the best is to book the ticket beforehand really great option i used it a lot good mix you know to just sit back and relax so you don't have to drive but still you know that you're going to leave on time when you go to the dead sea i recommend you staying there for a night you will probably go to the dead sea to enjoy it i mean it's fascinating you know to float there to rub yourself with the mud but there's a few things to be aware of first of all the public beach where the buses usually drop you off you have to pay entrance and it's around 20 25 quite expensive and the mud is not included there's no food included there's a lot of people it didn't really feel that nice to be there now i've heard from other people who went to the resorts that are there you know you can pay for day pass in one of those nice hotel resorts they have their private beach there's mother usually food is included and it costs around 35 40 so it's not that much more if you take into consideration that you also take food at the public beach so my best tip for you is to go to one of those resorts and book a day pass there best best best is to stay a night so you can really enjoy it in the night you know you can just go to your room you shower you can go to the dead sea at night as well you can enjoy it without a lot of tourists and people being there just with the people in your hotel so that's the best option to maybe just stay a night there now there is an option for a free place and it's behind the public beach but you have to walk a little bit and so there you don't have to pay everything is for free just be aware that the buses don't drop you off at this part so you kind of have to google maybe ask the locals and walk a little bit further to get to that point but yeah i mean if you want you can definitely go there for free as well you know and then head back to amman with one of the buses when you go to petra please wake up early to be there sharp at 6 00 a.m to be there before all the tourists arrive now the big buses with all the tourists they usually come at like 9am and then it's gonna be a completely different experience than when you come early and just have it for yourself it's it's magical it's really beautiful and i recommend you to be there early six am now i recommend when you're there go off a bit of the beaten path there is two trails that are off the main trail one is called the vat alfa rasa trail it's really nice you you know go a bit up the hill you have a beautiful view you maybe have the case for yourselves there and another one is the treasury viewpoint trail very beautiful do that right away when you arrive in the morning you go there you have a beautiful view over the treasury most likely there's not going to be a lot of people because afterwards it's just crowded now another thing you can do is petra by night it's really really beautiful it's petra during the night and they put up some candles and just you know the experience of being there at night very magical but keep in mind you have to buy an extra ticket on top so it's not included in the jordan pass or the normal day pass you have to get a petra by night ticket it costs around 24 some people say it's worth it some people say it's not worth it i haven't done it because all the tickets were booked out but i heard from a lot of people that they just loved it and just the experience of being there by night you know the stars you sit in front of the treasury that's magical and go there late you know just right before it starts because then you may be one of the last ones who go through the canyon that's a very magical experience by itself just you know the canyon lit by the candles when you go to the buddy rum desert you're probably gonna stay in one of those bedouin camps now please don't do it like me to just show up there and book your stuff there book everything beforehand book the hotel book the tour you're gonna do the jeep ride whatever you have in mind do that before because when you arrive there they will pick you up every camp will pick you up at the starting point and then they will bring you to the camp you know and when you arrive there and you haven't booked anything you know you will have to book it there and then you pretty much are in the hands of the owner because he can just dictate a price and you can just hop to another camp because you can't get there you know there's just a jeep of the camp that would have to bring you back to the starting point and then another one would have to pick you up so book all this stuff beforehand so you know the price people can't scam you and if you're a solo traveler maybe you know you meet some others where you can arrange it together because that pulls down the price one of the first questions people might say is oh you're going to jordan is that even safe and yes it is absolutely safe i can tell you no problem at all there's a lot of tours coming there every every year you won't have any problems you might be a little bit overwhelmed because you know of the area of craziness that's definitely for sure i mean i had that when i arrived it's just a craziness that's different to what we're used to now the only thing you should be aware of is scams and that's you know normal in a lot of tourist places especially in petra now there are so many tourists coming and there's going to be a lot of scams you know people trying to sell you something they maybe put like a wristband on you to say it's for free and then it's not for free they're the horses that bring you to petra where you have to go through the canyon they say that's for free it is technically for free but in the end they want a hefty tip so don't do the horses just be aware of the scams another thing that's really dangerous in jordan is the traffic so be aware in a month especially when you cross the streets people drive a bit crazier there so what i learned you know just put out the hand that's kind of like a sign like i'm coming please slow down a bit that kind of helps and you know just maybe look for local that you can go behind so yeah just be aware of scams and the traffic everything else fine you're going to be completely completely safe now when it comes to clothing it's a middle eastern country so of course women tend to dress a bit more conservative now i don't want you to worry you know wear your things you can wear shorts you can wear tank tops especially in those beach side cities and resorts i mean it's no problem at all but just be aware that it is a muslim country and you want to be respectful now the only thing is when you want to visit religious sites such as mosque your knees have to be covered up your shoulders women will have to wear a headscarf everything else completely fine and the last thing the last tip i can give you is do a free walking tour when you're in amman great great thing because you can really experience the city by a local and you go to places that you normally won't go to i mean the one i did there's plenty but the one i did was the one with muhammad izzat and you know he's born in the city he's lived there his whole life so it was really interesting to get the view of the city by a local and he took us to some places like the gun market where i normally would have never went so really do that because you get a really great insight of what the city is about and what makes it live and breathe and why it's so special to the locals overall you're gonna have a fantastic time in jordan it's a great country great people fantastic food and if you you know keep those little tricks and tips in mind i guarantee you you will have a fantastic vacation

Amman is one of the biggest tourist attractions in Jordan having many best places in Amman. Amman, the capital of Jordan, is a modern city with numerous ancient ruins. Atop Jabal al-Qala’a hill, the historic Citadel includes the pillars of the Roman Temple of Hercules and the 8th-century Umayyad Palace complex, known for its grand dome. Built into a different downtown hillside, the Roman Theater is a 6,000-capacity, 2nd-century stone amphitheater offering occasional events.

As Amman famous places has such a long history and covers a lot of ground, it means that there is a huge amount to see and do – but as best places in Amman is so well connected even if you only have one day in this city, because of a layover or a connecting flight, you can really pack in a lot. To help you even more, you can get a Amman beautiful places 1-Day Ticket.

videos.transcript: [Music] whether you're traveling to jordan to explore the ruins of petra or go glamping in wadi rum don't overlook the charming city of oman the hilly capital of jordan boasts countless attractions and places to visit that connect tourists with the local culture and get them excited for what else the country has to offer it's an underrated destination worth at least a few days in your itinerary number one amman citadel you don't have to go far to see amazing archaeological ruins in jordan's capital perched atop the highest hill in the city the amman citadel is conveniently located downtown and offers incredible views of the destination but more than just a lookout point the citadel is home to historical attractions that date as far back as the bronze age number two roman theater when it comes to things to do in amman swinging by the roman theater is high on the list and for good reason the antique attraction perfectly blends historic jordanian heritage with the city's vibrant modern day life a 20-minute walk from the amman citadel the restored roman theater deepens tourists understanding of what amman may have been like when the city went by the name philadelphia nearly two thousand years ago number three rainbow street rainbow street is the place to see and be seen in amman the famous road which can be found in the historic district of jabalaman offers a relaxing space for pedestrians to escape iman's chaotic traffic and explore a part of the capitol by foot number for royal automobile museum whether you're a die hard car enthusiast or you have a passion for road trips you'll find it easy to enjoy the royal automobile museum in the northern suburbs of amman established in 2003 the relatively young museum showcases former king of jordan hussein bin talal's rare cavalcade of cars and other vehicles from around the world number five jordan museum trip to amman is complete without a thorough look at the jordan museum's illuminating exhibitions the collection at this world-class institution located in a modern building next to city hall brings jordanian culture and history to life starting with the country's earliest known inhabitants number six amman's best falafel where to find the best falafel in amman is a hotly contested debate there are tons of local restaurants that churn out piles of the famous chickpea fritters and you can easily eat your way through the city trying to find your favorite falafel joint number seven among spas while the dead sea is the preeminent region for relaxing in jordan amman holds its own on the wellness travel scene with a number of excellent spas in fact the city's hammums still maintain bathing traditions that are hundreds of years old many of the facilities feature middle eastern style saunas steam rooms communal baths and private treatment rooms along with pleasant spaces to wind down number eight kazar al-abidi about 20 kilometers west of amman stands one of the few examples of hellenistic architecture in the country kazar al-abidi the desert palace which was built from some of the largest blocks in the middle east features two stories that survived a massive earthquake in the year 362. number nine jordan national gallery of fine arts if you want to see what middle eastern art is all about head to the jordan national gallery of fine arts the small but mighty museum showcases works from around the world with particular attention to jordan and its surrounding region the permanent collection contains more than 2 800 works including paintings multimedia creations photography weaving and sculptures and represent artists from more than 60 countries number 10 amongst other ancient attractions the amon citadel and the roman theater aren't the only ancient attractions in jordan's bustling capital continue your journey back through time by visiting some of the other archaeological gems scattered around ammon just outside the roman theater tourists can see the few columns that remain of what was once one of imperial rome's largest public plazas the forum hope you like this video for more videos please subscribe to our channel

videos.title: Driving in Jordan from Amman to Aqaba, is it safe? (w/ tips & prices)
videos.description:
Driving in Jordan may seem to be intimidating, especially if you are planning to drive from Amman to Aqaba. However, renting a car in Jordan every easy and affordable and it allows you to visit the country without worrying about transports time schedules etc. Fuel in Jordan is very affordable (compared to Europe) and Jordanians are very friendly so they will be happy to help if you have any problems or are unsure about how to go about it.

My first drive in Jordan was quite stressful and admittedly I was a bit nervous, but it didn't take long for me to become confident with driving in Jordan and I ended up really enjoying it. I would recommend to visit Amman first and perhaps take one of the Amman walking tours offered by your accommodation and then drive to Aqaba via the Desert Highway, the King's Highway or the Jordan Valley Highway.
tags: is it safe to drive in jordan

videos.title: Jordan travel advice from a Jordanian, DON'T make my same mistake! (Jordan Travel Vlog)
videos.description:
Getting from Amman Airport to Amman City is and adventure in itself and so it was really worth making this Jordan travel vlog in Amman airport. In the Jordan airport of Amman I was lucky to meet a real Jordanian that gave me many useful travel tips for Jordan that I hope you will find useful too. And he was even so kind to negotiate the taxi fare for me. Honestly, Jordanian people are just the best!

This is the hotel where I stayed: http://www.zamanyazamanhotel.com

This is the link to buy your Jordan Pass: https://www.jordanpass.jo

I shot this video on my DJI Pocket 2: https://www.dji.com/uk/pocket-2?site=brandsite&from=nav

All music is from Epidemic Sound: https://www.epidemicsound.com/music/featured/

In this video, we’ll be taking you through some of the best things to do in Amman
Our first stop is the ancient Citadel, which sits atop a hill and offers stunning panoramic views of the city
Next up, we have the Roman Amphitheatre, which is a must-visit for history buffs
Take a walk around and soak up the atmosphere or climb up to the top for a bird's eye view of the surrounding area
Make sure to haggle for the best deals
If you're looking for some relaxation, a visit to the Turkish Bathhouses is highly recommended
Finally, we have the Jordan Museum, which is a must-visit for anyone interested in the history of Jordan
